text: fat is 30.7g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 42.1g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for a seed, the best ones punch way above their size.
protein is 16.5g. the amino acid profile is carrying real weight. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 3% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
magnesium at 80% DV, zinc at 42% DV, iron at 43% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

the upsides first. extreme fibre density because adds standout digestive and satiety value even beyond the headline nutrient display. can be very filling for the amount used because the gel-forming fibre profile gives it practical fullness value. easy to add into meals because can be mixed into oats, yogurt, puddings, and smoothies without much friction.

on the weaker side. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. texture can put some people off because the gel-like texture is not universally appealing. usually works best with some preparation because often needs soaking or mixing into something else to shine.
Chia Seeds is S tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
